iti34:1.2: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti34:1.3: "A person without ardour, without concern [for the results of doing evil], is incapable of self-awakening, incapable of Unbinding, incapable of attaining the unsurpassed safety from bondage. A person ardent and concerned is capable of self-awakening, capable of Unbinding, capable of attaining the unsurpassed safety from bondage."
iti34:1.4: With no ardour, no concern, lazy, with low persistence, full of sloth and drowsiness, shameless, without respect: he's incapable, a monk like this, of touching superlative self-awakening.
iti34:1.5: But whoever is mindful, masterful, absorbed in jhana, ardent, concerned, and heedful, cutting the fetter of birth and aging, touches right here a self-awakening un-surpassed.
iti34:1.6: This, too, was the meaning of what was said by the Blessed One, so I have heard.
